question: [File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single HTTP request to /api/v1/balances with the maximum allowed `limit` parameter combined with a timestamp filter, forcing three sequential database round-trips (getTsQuery, getAccountIdsByPublicKey, pool.queryQuietly) per request, causing sustained DB connection pool exhaustion and service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply many repeated `timestamp=ne:X` query parameters, causing `neParams` array to grow unboundedly and the resulting `not consensus_timestamp = any ($4)` clause to pass a massive array to PostgreSQL, exhausting query planning memory and causing DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a large number of `timestamp=gte:X` and `timestamp=lte:Y` parameters, causing `tsQuery.split('?')` to produce a large array and the `forEach` loop with `BigInt()` conversion to consume excessive CPU per request, enabling a single-threaded Node.js event loop stall?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountIdsByPublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a secondary database query by supplying a valid `account.publickey` parameter, and by sending many concurrent requests each triggering `pool.queryQuietly(entityPublicKeyQuery, params)`, exhaust the PostgreSQL connection pool and deny service to all other API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:X` parameter where X is a very large nanosecond value, causing `getAccountBalanceTimestampRange` to execute a full sequential scan of the `account_balance` table (no matching partition pruning), resulting in a long-running query that holds a DB connection and degrades service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the correlated `getTokenBalanceSubQuery` subquery (executed once per row in the result set) by requesting the maximum `limit` with a timestamp filter, causing O(limit) correlated subquery executions per request and multiplying DB load proportionally to the number of concurrent attackers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the correlated `getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery` (used in the no-timestamp path) by sending requests without a timestamp filter, causing one correlated subquery against `token_account` per result row, and by sending many concurrent requests exhaust DB CPU and deny service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseAccountIdQueryParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply many `account.id=X` parameters in a single request, causing `parseAccountIdQueryParam` to build a large SQL `IN (?, ?, ...)` clause, which when executed causes PostgreSQL to perform an expensive index scan over a large set of IDs, degrading query performance and enabling DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseAccountIdQueryParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a single valid EVM address as `account.id`, triggering an async `EntityService.getEncodedId()` DB lookup inside `Promise.all(accountParamsPromise)`, and by sending many concurrent requests each triggering this extra DB call, exhaust the connection pool and cause DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseAccountIdQueryParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid account alias as `account.id`, triggering `EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias()` which performs a DB lookup, and by sending many concurrent requests each triggering this extra DB call, exhaust the connection pool and cause sustained DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user combine `account.publickey`, `timestamp`, and `account.id` (EVM alias) parameters in a single request to force four sequential or parallel DB queries (entityPublicKeyQuery, account_balance timestamp range, alias resolution, main balances query), maximizing per-request DB load and enabling DoS with fewer concurrent connections?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes `getBalancesQuery` to build a SQL query with `distinct on (account_id)` over the full `account_balance` table partition, where the `order by ab.account_id ${order}, ab.consensus_timestamp desc` requires a full sort, consuming excessive PostgreSQL memory and CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: formatBalancesResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response with the maximum number of rows, causing `rows.map()` and the nested `utils.parseTokenBalances(row.token_balances)` to execute for every row in Node.js, consuming excessive CPU in the event loop and blocking other requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: formatBalancesResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where `rows.map((r) => r.consensus_timestamp).reduce(...)` iterates over the maximum number of rows, and by sending many concurrent requests cause sustained CPU exhaustion in the Node.js process, degrading service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `consensusTsQuery.replaceAll('ab.', 'tb.')` to process a very long query string (from many timestamp parameters), consuming CPU proportional to string length and causing event loop delays when many requests are processed concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests without any timestamp filter, causing the `entity_balance` CTE path to execute a full scan of the `entity` table filtered by `type in ('ACCOUNT', 'CONTRACT')` with a correlated `getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery` per row, resulting in a very expensive query that holds a DB connection for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `timestamp=ne:X` parameters, causing `neParams` to accumulate many values that are passed as `$4` array to the `not consensus_timestamp = any ($4)` clause, forcing PostgreSQL to evaluate a large exclusion set per row scanned, degrading query performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=gte:0` parameter (minimum value), causing `lowerBound` to remain 0n and `getAccountBalanceTimestampRange` to scan the widest possible timestamp range in the `account_balance` table, resulting in a full partition scan and long-running DB query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:9223372036854775807` (MAX_LONG) parameter, causing `upperBound` to be set to the maximum BigInt value and `effectiveUpperBound` to be capped at `nowInNs()`, but still causing `getAccountBalanceTimestampRange` to scan a two-month partition range, resulting in an expensive DB query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where `tsParams` is `[upper, lower, upper, lower, upper]` (5 parameters) and the resulting SQL query with `distinct on (account_id)` over a large timestamp range causes PostgreSQL to perform an expensive sort-based deduplication, consuming significant memory and CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send many concurrent requests each with a valid `account.publickey` parameter, causing each request to execute `entityPublicKeyQuery` against the `entity` table, and the combined load of these queries to exhaust PostgreSQL CPU and connection slots, denying service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountIdsByPublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid public key that matches many entities in the database, causing `getAccountIdsByPublicKey` to return a large `ids` array and build a large `ab.account_id in (...)` SQL clause, which when used in the main query causes an expensive multi-value IN scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getBalances` performs `await Promise.all(accountParamsPromise)` which may resolve multiple async DB lookups (EVM address + alias) concurrently, and by sending many such requests simultaneously, exhaust the DB connection pool faster than single-lookup requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with `account.balance=gte:0` (matching all accounts) combined with maximum `limit`, causing the main query to return the maximum number of rows each with a correlated token balance subquery, maximizing both DB and Node.js CPU load per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `select distinct on (token_id)` subquery inside `getTokenBalanceSubQuery` for each account row returned, where each subquery scans `token_balance` filtered by `account_id` and a timestamp range, causing O(limit) expensive subquery executions per request?
